title: lathyrus oleraceus subsp. biflorus
intro: Lathyrus oleraceus subsp. biflorus, also known as the broad-leaved vetch or winter pea, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Lathyrus oleraceus subsp. biflorus include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, lower cholesterol levels, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus oleraceus subsp. biflorus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support heart health and promote relaxation.
